title: 10 possible side effects of Calendula officinalis (Calendula) if used improperly
intro: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, is a plant that has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, but if used improperly, it can have several potential health side effects. First, ingesting large amounts of the plant may lead to stomach discomfort, nausea, and vomiting. Additionally, contact with the skin can cause irritation, particularly in those with sensitive skin. In rare cases, an allergic reaction may occur, resulting in symptoms such as skin rash, hives, and difficulty breathing. Moreover, calendula should not be used internally during pregnancy, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and lead to miscarriage. Finally, it may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and sedatives, reducing their effectiveness or increasing their side effects. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before using calendula, especially for those with pre-existing medical conditions or those taking medications.
